entities.name: spiritus aerialis
entities.raw_quote: When a spiritus aerialis appeared, at Bunwell, they promised it the body of a Christian man in exchange for its leading them to treasure

entities.name: Floron

attributes.appearance_in_mirror: armed knight seated on a horse, and his squire accompanying him

entities.raw_quote: The master... will see an armed knight seated on a horse, and his squire accompanying him.

entities.name: Mirror of Floron
entities.raw_quote: After it has been made, it should be anointed with pure and bright balsam, and fumigated with aloes, ambergris, myrrh and white frankincense.

entities.name: Lilith
entities.raw_quote: The First Mirror of Lilith (spelled Lilit or Lylet) is dedicated to this ancient Hebrew demon and to her followers and her ‘knights’

entities.name: Fingernail Ritual

tools: left thumb nail of a virgin boy

entities.raw_quote: take a virgin boy of legitimate birth... scrape well the thumb nail of his left hand with a knife. Then bind to the same thumb, beneath the nail, a slip with the following names written on it.

entities.name: virgin boy

attributes.requirement: must be able to see spirits; often young and virginal

entities.raw_quote: the medium, usually a young boy, is vital; it is he alone who actually sees the spirits that are conjured.

summary: This text describes various divinatory experiments from the Munich manuscript (Clm 849), focusing on scrying techniques using mirrors, crystals, and fingernails. It details specific rituals like the Mirror of Floron and a complex fingernail-based ritual used to detect theft or find information.
key_passages: The master... should sit and have this mirror held with both hands by a virgin boy before his chest.
The First Mirror of Lilith (spelled Lilit or Lylet) is dedicated to this ancient Hebrew demon and to her followers and her ‘knights’
the medium, usually a young boy, is vital; it is he alone who actually sees the spirits that are conjured.
take a virgin boy of legitimate birth... scrape well the thumb nail of his left hand with a knife.
The master... should look in the mirror, and he will see an armed knight seated on a horse, and his squire accompanying him.
